description: FactoryOS evaluation scenarios for the AI Platform analysis pipeline. Each scenario includes an incident input, expected characteristics of the AI output, and evaluation criteria.

scenarios.name: CNC Bearing Failure (High Severity)

input.title: CNC Machine M-12 Bearing Failure — Bay 3
input.description: Machine M-12 (CNC Milling Machine) in Bay 3, Line A stopped production due to a suspected spindle bearing failure. A loud grinding noise was observed for 3 days before the machine tripped on thermal overload and shut down. The machine was producing AEC-4412-B for Aerospace Components Ltd. with a critical deadline of July 8, 2026.

scenarios.notes: The canonical M-12 demo scenario. Used for demonstrations and baseline evaluation.

scenarios.name: Coolant System Failure (Critical Severity)

input.title: Primary Coolant Loop Failure — Production Line B
input.description: The primary coolant circulation pump for Production Line B has seized. Multiple CNC machines are shutting down on thermal overload. Coolant temperature reached 95°C before automatic shutoff. Estimated 8 machines affected. Safety officer has been notified. Risk of damage to precision spindles and bearings if machines are restarted without coolant flow.

scenarios.notes: Critical severity — should always require human approval.

scenarios.name: Inventory Shortage (Medium Severity)

input.title: Critical Parts Shortage — Bearing SKF 6205
input.description: Stock of SKF 6205 bearings (critical spare part for CNC spindles) has dropped to 2 units, below the minimum reorder threshold of 10 units. Lead time from primary supplier is 3-4 weeks. Two machines are currently scheduled for preventive bearing replacement next week.

scenarios.notes: Supply chain scenario — Inventory Agent should be the primary contributor.

scenarios.name: Production Deadline Risk (High Severity)

input.title: Production Deadline Risk — Aerospace Order AEC-4412-B
input.description: Machine M-12 failure has put the Aerospace Components Ltd. order AEC-4412-B at risk. The order is for 500 precision milled parts with a contractual delivery deadline of July 8, 2026. As of today, only 320 parts have been completed. The remaining 180 parts require 45 hours of CNC machining time. Penalty clauses of $50,000/day apply for late delivery.

scenarios.notes: Combines production and cost scenarios. Both Production and Cost agents should be primary contributors.

scenarios.name: Minor Sensor Fault (Low Severity)

input.title: Temperature Sensor Fault — Machine M-03
input.description: Machine M-03 is reporting erratic temperature readings from the spindle temperature sensor. The sensor appears to be giving intermittent false high readings (130°C when normal operating temperature is 45°C). The machine has been configured to ignore the faulty sensor and is currently operating normally. This is a low-priority maintenance item.

scenarios.notes: Low severity scenario. Should not require human approval. Economical plan should be the most obvious recommendation.

evaluation_metrics.schema_compliance: All OrchestratorResult fields present and correctly typed
evaluation_metrics.plan_completeness: All three plan types (economical, rapid, balanced) generated
evaluation_metrics.executive_summary_quality: Non-empty executive summary with at least 50 characters
evaluation_metrics.agent_participation: At least 3 agents consulted per analysis
evaluation_metrics.cost_ordering: Economical plan should generally have lowest cost estimate
evaluation_metrics.time_ordering: Rapid plan should generally have lowest downtime estimate
